title: RFC 9700 Phase 6 + Phase 5 Remainder — Complete Spec Compliance
description:
Master epic tracking completion of all remaining OAuth2/OIDC spec audit work. Scope per user directive (b1): all Phase 5 open items + full Phase 6 RFC 9700 Security BCP hardening = 22 unique subtasks across 7 waves (Wave 0 foundation → Wave 6 draft extensions).

AUTHORITATIVE SOURCES:
- docs/oauth2-spec-audit.md §8 (Phase 5 open) and §9 (Phase 6 full)
- §9.3 conformance test vectors (13 required) must all pass in tests/rfc9700_compliance.rs

VERIFICATION GATE (every subtask):
- cargo fmt --all -- --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test --verbose --all-features --locked
- New/updated vectors in tests/rfc9700_compliance.rs per §9.3 where applicable

PROJECT CONSTRAINTS (from CLAUDE.md — every worker must honor):
- TokenActor::new(storage, jwt_secret, issuer) signature changes touch 4 test call sites (tests/opaque_tokens.rs, tests/security_http.rs ×2, tests/device_flow.rs) + crates/oauth2-server/src/lib.rs
- Claims::new(subject, client_id, scope, duration_seconds, issuer) — issuer is 5th param
- RFC tests: build App inline per test (raw tuple helper), never return impl Service<actix_http::Request>
- DB schema changes: add V{n}__*.sql + update both sqlx (sqlite+postgres branches) AND mongo backends
- jsonwebtoken is production dep (OIDC logout id_token_hint validation)
- Prefer sqlite::memory: for RFC tests

WAVE DEPENDENCIES:
- Wave 0 (foundation: test harness + small refactors) MUST finish before Wave 1+
- Waves 1–3 may run parallel internally (mostly independent files)
- Wave 4 depends on Wave 0/1 deliverables
- Waves 5–6 last (OIDC session + draft extensions, lowest priority)

Deduplications: 5.8 ≡ 6.13 (native apps), 5.10 ≡ 6.6 (state enforcement) — tracked once each.

title: [Wave0] 6.14 Scaffold tests/rfc9700_compliance.rs conformance harness (13 §9.3 vectors, initial stubs allowed)
[Wave0] 6.7 Change login POST redirect 302 → 303 See Other (RFC 9700 §4.11)
[Wave0] 6.11 Introspection PII scoping — no username leak across clients (RFC 7662 §5)
[Wave1] 6.1 Revoke token family on authorization-code replay (RFC 9700 §2.1.5)
[Wave1] 6.4 Configurable token TTLs + refresh absolute-max cap (RFC 9700 §2.3, §4.14)
[Wave1] 6.3 Wire aud claim to RFC 8707 resource parameter (RFC 9068)
[Wave1] 6.5 JWT client-assertion jti replay store (RFC 7523 §3, RFC 9700 §2.5)
[Wave2] 6.2 DPoP proof validation on /token /introspect /refresh (RFC 9449)
[Wave2] 6.10 mTLS client authentication — tls_client_auth + self_signed_tls_client_auth (RFC 8705)
[Wave2] 6.12 JAR/private_key_jwt client jwks_uri with TTL cache (RFC 9101, 7523)
[Wave3] 6.6/5.10 Per-client require_state policy flag + enforcement (RFC 9700 §4.7)
[Wave3] 6.9 Rate limiting defaults on /oauth/token + /device/token, separate invalid_client bucket (RFC 9700 §2.5, RFC 8628 §3.5)
[Wave3] 6.8 App-layer TLS enforcement + HSTS header (RFC 9700 §2.6)
[Wave4] 5.4 JWT Introspection Response finish — application/token-introspection+jwt (RFC 9701)
[Wave3] 6.13/5.8 RFC 8252 Native Apps — loopback + custom URI redirect validation
[Wave4] 5.5 JWK Thumbprint URI support (RFC 9278)
[Wave4] 5.9 OAuth 2.1 compliance audit + gap report
[Wave5] 5.11 OIDC Session Management 1.0 — check_session_iframe + session_state
[Wave5] 5.13 OIDC Back-Channel Logout 1.0 — logout_token + per-client URI
[Wave5] 5.12 OIDC Front-Channel Logout 1.0
[Wave6] 5.15 Transaction Tokens (draft-ietf-oauth-transaction-tokens)
[Wave6] 5.14 Attestation-Based Client Authentication (draft-ietf-oauth-attestation-based-client-auth)
